page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-8 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the n4 server family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical Intel processor cores with 2 threads per core. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 260 MB of L3 cache. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark evaluations place the n4-standard-8 primarily in the average performance tier across most compute workloads. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1770.0 and a multi-core score of 6838.0. Text processing performance is a relative strength, scoring 2315.0 in Geekbench, which falls in the top 25% category. However, memory subsystem performance presents a notable tradeoff; the Passmark memory score of 1809.35 is categorized in the bottom 25%. Large block uncached memory read bandwidth is measured at 61.33 GB/sec, and write bandwidth is 37.65 GB/sec.

For specialized tasks, the hardware supports hardware-accelerated matrix operations via AMX flags. In LLM inference benchmarks using a small 135M parameter model, the server processes prompts at 642.95 tokens/sec and generates text at 180.36 tokens/sec. For a medium 7B model, prompt processing reaches 49.59 tokens/sec, but text generation drops to a weak 6.31 tokens/sec. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the n4-standard-8 offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads that do not require high-speed local NVMe storage or high-bandwidth memory performance. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and general-purpose applications.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-8 is an x86_64 general-purpose server featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs from 4 physical Intel cores and 32.0 GB of RAM. It includes 260 MB of L3 cache and supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instructions. The instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs. Performance benchmarks generally fall within the average tier, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 1770.0 and a multi-core score of 6838.0. Text processing is a relative strength, scoring in the top 25%, whereas memory performance is a clear tradeoff, with a Passmark memory score in the bottom 25%. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth is 61.33 GB/sec. This server is suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and light database operations where high memory bandwidth is not a critical requirement.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-8 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 8 Intel vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and AMX support, delivering balanced compute performance with a tradeoff in memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-8 is an x86_64 server with 8 Intel vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and AMX support, suited for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel processors with advanced matrix extensions and balanced compute-to-memory resources.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel physical cores with hyper-threading.
32 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector and matrix operations.
Strong text processing performance, scoring in the top 25% of benchmarks.
Tradeoff in memory performance with a Passmark memory score in the bottom 25%.
Suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and software compilation.
